Class Flash

java.lang.Object
neqsim.thermodynamicoperations.BaseOperation
neqsim.thermodynamicoperations.flashops.Flash
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Runnable, OperationInterface
Direct Known Subclasses:
CalcIonicComposition, CriticalPointFlash, ImprovedVUflashQfunc, OptimizedVUflash, PHflash, PHflashGERG2008, PHflashLeachman, PHflashSingleComp, PHflashVega, PHsolidFlash, PSflashSingleComp, PUflash, PVFflash, PVrefluxflash, QfuncFlash, TPflash, TPgradientFlash, TVflash, TVfractionFlash, VHflash, VHflashQfunc, VSflash, VUflash, VUflashQfunc, VUflashSingleComp
public abstract class Flash extends BaseOperation
Abstract base class for all flash classes.
Author:
Even Solbraa
See Also:

      Resets the diagnostic fields before a new stability decision is attempted.

    • recordStabilityOutcome

      protected void recordStabilityOutcome(String outcome)
      Records the high-level outcome of the most recent stability decision.
      Parameters:
      outcome - short text describing the stability path and decision

    • recordStabilityAnalysisFailure

      protected void recordStabilityAnalysisFailure(Exception exception)
      Records that a stability calculation failed and stores its diagnostic message.
      Parameters:
      exception - exception raised during stability analysis or stability gating

      stability outcome text

    • hasLastStabilityAnalysisFailed

      public boolean hasLastStabilityAnalysisFailed()
      Checks whether the most recent stability analysis failed before reaching a decision.
      Returns:
      true if the last stability analysis or stability gate threw an exception

    • getLastStabilityAnalysisFailureMessage

      public String getLastStabilityAnalysisFailureMessage()
      Gets the failure message from the most recent failed stability analysis.
      Returns:
      failure message, or an empty string if no failure has been recorded

    • getLastTangentPlaneDistances

      public double[] getLastTangentPlaneDistances()
      Gets the tangent-plane-distance values from the most recent stability analysis.
      Returns:
      copy of the recorded tangent-plane-distance values

    • findLowestGibbsEnergyPhase

      public int findLowestGibbsEnergyPhase()

      findLowestGibbsEnergyPhase.

      Returns:
      a int

    • amplifiedKStabilityRetry

      private boolean amplifiedKStabilityRetry()
      Retry stability analysis with amplified Wilson K-values using an independent system clone. Called when the standard stability analysis declares the system stable, to catch near-critical instability (e.g. near the cricondenbar where Wilson K values approach 1 and standard trials converge to trivial solutions).

      Only runs when Wilson K-values indicate near-critical conditions (trial sums close to 1.0), avoiding false positives in clearly non-critical systems.

      Returns:
      true if instability was found (K-values on system are updated)

    • stabilityAnalysis

      public void stabilityAnalysis() throws IsNaNException, TooManyIterationsException

      stabilityAnalysis.

      Throws:
      IsNaNException - if any.
      TooManyIterationsException - if any.

    • pureComponentStabilityTrials

      protected boolean pureComponentStabilityTrials()
      Tries pure-component trial phases to detect liquid-liquid equilibrium (LLE) instability.

      The standard stabilityAnalysis() uses Wilson K-value based initial guesses, which assume gas-liquid equilibrium (GLE). At temperatures well below the critical temperature of the lightest component, Wilson K-values converge to values less than 1 for all components, making them ineffective for detecting LLE. This method complements the standard analysis by initializing trial phases as nearly-pure heaviest and lightest components, which is the same approach used by TPmultiflash.

      Returns:
      true if LLE instability was detected and K-values have been set on the system

    • shouldRunAutomaticLLECheck

      protected boolean shouldRunAutomaticLLECheck()
      Determine whether automatic LLE supplementary stability checks should be enabled.

      Automatic checks are enabled for systems where LLE/VLLE is common even if users have not explicitly turned on setMultiPhaseCheck(true).

      Returns:
      true if automatic LLE checks should run

    • shouldApplyEnhancedMultiPhaseCheck

      protected boolean shouldApplyEnhancedMultiPhaseCheck()
      Determines whether enhanced multiphase stability numerics should be applied.

      Enhanced checks are intended for systems where ordinary Wilson K-value stability analysis is known to miss polar, associating, electrolyte, or sour-fluid liquid splits. Hydrocarbon-only PR/SRK mixtures are handled by the ordinary multiphase flash path; applying the enhanced numerical filters there can create isolated phase-map artifacts near phase boundaries.

      Returns:
      true if enhanced stability checks are enabled and the active mixture contains components that need the enhanced path

    • isEnhancedStabilityComponent

      private boolean isEnhancedStabilityComponent(String lowerComponentName)
      Checks whether a component name is associated with enhanced multiphase stability behavior.
      Parameters:
      lowerComponentName - component name converted to lower case
      Returns:
      true if the component should keep enhanced multiphase stability checks active

      stabilityCheck.

      Returns:
      a boolean
